channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti vandalism, 3rd June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southampton Way, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous 'lump' on pathway, 3rd June
item.description:
There is a lump on the pathway leading from the Betts Way entrance into Betts Park. A lady tripped on this 'lump' and got quite badly hurt. This 'lump' needs to be flattened before other people trip on it and hurt themselves.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Betts Way, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter bin overflowing - yet again, 3rd June
item.description:
Once again I have to tell you that the litter bin near the entrance to Kelsey Park in Stone Park Avenue is full and overflowing. I previously had to bring the same bin to your attention on 6 May and then again on 17 May. Surely the contractor should empty it often enough for me to have make reports like this one

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 39, Stone Park Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole debris flying onto pavement, 3rd June
item.description:
2 weeks old
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, Elm Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped freezers at south end of Prior Street, 3rd June
item.description:
These look like two fly- tipped freezers orobably from a flat in the Council block shown behind, probably within the last two days. Council tenants (at the least) should be issued with visible notices/ stickers to put on items like this for which they may have made prior arrangement (and everyone should) for Council or other collection. This is the second fly-tipping incident I have reported on the same spot within two weeks.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Royal Hill, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 trials bikes no registration numbers being ridden on the roads and paths causing public safety issues, 3rd June
item.description:
Continuing issue of dangerous riding of trials bikes and electric bikes on paths and roads with no number plates.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Car, 3rd June
item.description:
Abandoned car, no insurance, no tax and no mot. Young men are using the car as storage attracting ASB.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queens Drive, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars parked in the cycle lane, 3rd June
item.description:
No lines to say no parking.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 130, South Eden Park Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car in church car park, 3rd June
item.description:
The car has been abandoned in the church car park over 2 months. There is a sign on the dashboard saying it has broken down, and will be collected tomorrow. However, it has been here for months. A dvla check shows its MOT ran out in January. The car has been abandoned and needs to be removed.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Court Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside No 30 Lisburne road NW3, 3rd June
item.description:
New tree had its fastenings cut or damaged . Tree is leaning precariously and somone eill be tempted to uproot it as gas happened before. Been loose about 3 weeks . Now close to being damaged.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lisburne Road, Gospel Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large bin left from last weeks collection on pavement, 3rd June
item.description:
Not gradually being filled with rubbish.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 94, Brabourne Rise, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
